mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 3% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS < 3% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses forward earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on RSAFS and HOUST interaction, or joint decline events produce positive returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 112 index points signals expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods with INDPRO > 112 do not produce significantly higher 5-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 100,000 persons signals labor market weakness that triggers risk-off sentiment and equity selloffs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite payroll decline, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3% year-over-year) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to activate.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or joint tightening episodes produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,100 thousand units signals robust residential investment demand that drives forward GDP growth expectations and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,100 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong housing demand
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST and 60-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods with HOUST > 1,100 do not produce significantly higher 60-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals supply-side contraction and reduced productive capacity that compresses corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the unemployment rate is simultaneously rising.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
regime_hint: supply shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on INDPRO and UNRATE interaction, or joint deterioration events produce positive returns in > 60% of cases.
